response.justification: While this is a technically skilled developer with 15+ years of diverse experience, the resume fails to meet critical job requirements. Most critically, there is no evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly listed as required. Additionally, the candidate's career has progressively moved away from embedded systems and C development (last C work in 2014-2015) toward high-level web application development. The role requires specialized embedded firmware security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption protocols, attack detection) for which there is no demonstrable background. The skill gaps are too significant to overcome.
strengths: Extensive software development experience across 15+ years with proven ability to learn diverse technology domains and languages
Solid Linux and infrastructure foundation with hands-on experience setting up CI/CD pipelines, GitLab instances, and deployment infrastructure
Strong commitment to code quality and testing practices, including creation of testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts) and emphasis on TDD across roles
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed on resume; job explicitly requires native-level Japanese—a hard requirement that is completely unmet
No embedded systems security domain expertise; resume shows no experience with required technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption protocols, attack detection, tamper detection)

C/embedded firmware skills are dormant; last significant C/C++ development was 2014-2015; recent 10 years focused on TypeScript, JavaScript, and high-level languages with clear career trajectory away from embedded systems
